purpose: Normalize payment-provider webhook payloads, verify idempotent reconciliation rules, and apply internal payment status transitions with audit-ready event metadata.

endpoints.description: Reconcile a normalized provider webhook payload against an internal payment record.

dependencies.purpose: Verify the webhook boundary before reconciliation is attempted

sidecar_specification.purpose: Idempotent reconciliation of payment-provider events into internal payment state.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use after webhook authenticity is established and a provider payload needs to be mapped onto internal payment and session state.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use for checkout creation, invoice generation, or card capture flows before a provider event exists.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Verify webhook signature. 2) Call /payments/reconcile with the provider payload. 3) Persist paid or unchanged state and record audit metadata.

failure_modes.condition: Payload cannot be normalized to a provider payment identifier or the payment record does not exist
failure_modes.recovery: Fix payload mapping, ensure the internal payment exists, and retry with the original event

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Normalize provider payload shapes first, look up the internal payment by provider identifier, short-circuit when already paid, and persist raw event metadata for auditability.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Different providers surface transaction identifiers and event identifiers in different fields, and duplicate deliveries must not re-apply state transitions.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Keep the reconciliation result deterministic and explicit about whether the event changed state or was idempotently ignored.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not call external provider APIs from the reconciler and do not bury webhook signature verification inside this module.

test_cases.name: already paid event returns idempotent response
